mnemonics.title: Break vs Continue
mnemonics.content:
BREAK = BREAK the loop completely
CONTINUE = CONTINUE to next iteration
Think: Break stops, Continue skips and continues

mnemonics.title: Labeled Break Remember
mnemonics.content:
LABELED BREAK: Look at the label (named point)
Unlabeled: Use U (Unlabeled = innermost/Underlying loop)
Labeled: Look L (Label = named loop you specify)

mnemonics.title: Return Rule
mnemonics.content:
RETURN: RE-ENTER calling method
R = Return to caller
E = Exit method immediately
T = Transfer control back

acronyms.usage: Remember the three main jump statements in Java

acronyms.expansion: Break Continue Return - remember their execution order in nested scenarios
acronyms.usage: When debugging loops, know BCR behavior

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing a loop, with an X mark showing where break exits the box completely
Draw a loop as a circle with an arrow, and show a curved arrow bypassing one point and returning to the main flow
Draw nested boxes (loops), label them outer and inner, show how labeled break jumps to exit outer box
Draw method as a function machine; return shows an exit pipe with arrow pointing back to source

analogies.analogy: Like exiting a building through an emergency exit - you leave the building (loop) immediately
Like skipping one song in a playlist and moving to the next song - you stay in the playlist (loop) but skip current item
Like leaving a meeting room entirely - you don't just exit the room, you leave the entire meeting (method)
Like using a master switch to turn off the main power, cutting electricity to all devices (nested loops) at once
